results.message: Displacement amount is above the configured production risk budget.
results.title: Displacement amount must stay within the risk budget
results.why: Excessive displacement can explode render time, memory, tessellation, and silhouette changes. Keeping amount within a profile-defined budget reduces farm risk.

results.message: Material displacement bounds span exceeds the configured budget.
results.title: Displacement bounds span must stay within budget
results.why: Wide displacement bounds increase silhouette change and memory cost. Tighter bounds keep farm renders predictable.

results.message: Material displacement risk score exceeds the configured budget.
results.title: Material displacement risk score must stay within budget
results.why: Weighted displacement risk combines amount, subdivision, bounds, and renderer flags into a single farm-risk signal for TD review.

results.message: Material uses displacement with subdivision enabled.
results.title: Displacement with subdivision requires review
results.why: Subdivision combined with displacement increases tessellation cost and farm memory pressure. These materials need TD review before publish.

results.message: Displacement texture file is missing or cannot be resolved.
results.title: Displacement texture file must exist
results.why: Missing displacement maps can change object silhouettes, create render holes, or make farm renders diverge from approved lookdev. Displacement dependencies must be resolved before publish or farm submission.

results.message: Material uses more high-cost shader nodes than the configured budget.
results.title: Material expensive node count must stay within budget
results.why: Layered, blend, and SSS shader nodes increase shading cost disproportionately. Counting expensive node types helps flag materials that may slow farm renders.

results.title: Material farm cost score must stay within budget
results.why: Weighted graph cost estimates aggregate node-type render expense. Materials above the budget are more likely to increase farm time and memory pressure.

results.why: Deep shader graphs are costly to inspect and can signal overly complex procedural or layered networks. Depth budgets help identify networks that need TD review.

results.message: Material shader graph has more nodes than the configured budget.
results.title: Material graph node count must stay within budget
results.why: Large shader graphs are harder to review, slower to evaluate, and more likely to hide expensive or broken branches. Budget checks keep material networks maintainable before publish.

results.why: Excessive texture count increases memory pressure, IO cost, review complexity, and farm risk. Keeping texture count within budget helps maintain predictable rendering cost.

results.message: Production geometry is assigned to the default material.
results.title: Production geometry should not use the default material
results.why: Default material assignments usually mean geometry lost its intended lookdev assignment. Detecting this prevents publishes with unshaded or fallback-looking production geometry.

results.message: Multiple materials share the same shading-network fingerprint.
results.title: Duplicate material shading networks should be reviewed
results.why: Duplicate material graphs increase scene complexity, memory use, and lookdev review cost. Reporting content-identical networks helps consolidate redundant shaders when assignments allow.

results.message: Scene exceeds duplicate-scan material or texture budget; duplicate detection may be truncated.
results.title: Duplicate scan budget exceeded for large scenes
results.why: Large scenes can contain hundreds of materials and thousands of texture nodes. Capping duplicate scans keeps validation responsive while still reporting duplicates inside the scanned subset.

results.message: Material has no assigned geometry.
results.title: Material should be assigned to production geometry
results.why: Unassigned materials are often dead shader networks. Reporting them keeps assets easier to review and prevents stale lookdev nodes from hiding in production scenes.

results.message: Color texture is configured as non-color data.
results.title: Color textures must use a color-managed color space
results.why: Base color, specular color, and emission maps represent color information. They should remain color-managed so viewport, lookdev, and final renders interpret them consistently.

results.message: Color texture is configured as non-color data.

results.message: Color texture is configured as non-color data.

results.message: Data texture uses a color-managed color space.
results.title: Data textures must use Raw color space
results.why: Scalar maps such as roughness, masks, normal, bump, displacement, and opacity must not be transformed as color data because color transforms alter numeric values.

results.message: Data texture uses a color-managed color space.
results.title: Data textures must use Raw color space

results.message: Multiple texture nodes reference the same resolved texture path.
results.title: Duplicate texture file usage should be reviewed
results.why: Duplicate texture nodes that point to the same file increase graph complexity and make material reviews harder. Reporting duplicate file usage helps consolidate redundant texture nodes when it is safe to do so.

results.message: Texture file is missing or cannot be resolved.
results.title: Texture file must exist
results.why: Missing texture files render incorrectly in Maya and on the render farm. Blocking them before publish prevents wasted farm time and broken lookdev handoffs.

results.message: Optimized texture derivative (.tx) is missing for a raster source texture.
results.title: Optimized texture derivative should exist for raster sources
results.why: Missing .tx derivatives increase render startup cost, texture IO, and farm memory pressure. Raster sources should have a matching optimized derivative when policy tracks them.

results.message: Optimized texture derivative (.tx) is missing for a raster source texture.
results.title: Optimized texture derivative should exist for raster sources
results.why: Missing .tx derivatives increase render startup cost, texture IO, and farm memory pressure. Raster sources should have a matching optimized derivative when policy tracks them.

results.message: Optimized texture derivative is stale compared with its source texture.
results.title: Optimized texture derivative should be fresh
results.why: Stale .tx derivatives can render older pixels than the source texture and make local lookdev diverge from farm rendering.

results.message: One or more UDIM .tx tiles are missing for a raster UDIM source texture.
results.title: UDIM optimized texture tiles should exist
results.why: Partial UDIM .tx coverage causes farm renders to fall back to heavy source tiles or fail tile lookup. All published UDIM source tiles should have matching .tx tiles.

results.message: Texture path points to a local drive and may not resolve on the farm.
results.title: Texture path must not use a local drive root
results.why: Local drive paths are machine-specific. Render farm workers and other artists may not be able to resolve them, causing missing textures or inconsistent renders.

results.message: Texture path is outside the approved project texture roots.
results.title: Texture path must use an approved project root
results.why: Project-rooted paths keep scene dependencies portable between artists, publish tools, and render farm workers. Paths outside approved roots are harder to audit and reproduce.

results.title: Texture path must use an approved project root
results.why: Project-rooted paths keep scene dependencies portable between artists, publish tools, and render farm workers. Paths outside approved roots are harder to audit and reproduce.

results.message: Texture path is outside the approved project texture roots.

results.message: Texture path points to a user-local folder such as Desktop, Downloads, or Temp.
results.title: Texture path must not use a user-local folder
results.why: User-local folders are not production storage. They are commonly unavailable to farm machines and are unsafe for publish handoff.

results.message: Background texture exceeds the configured resolution budget.
results.title: Background texture resolution budget
results.why: Background assets can use lower resolution tiers to keep scene memory and IO costs manageable.

results.message: Hero texture exceeds the configured resolution budget.
results.title: Hero texture resolution budget
results.why: Oversized hero textures increase memory pressure and farm render times without proportional quality gains.

results.message: Prop texture exceeds the configured resolution budget.
results.title: Prop texture resolution budget
results.why: Prop assets should stay within tiered texture budgets so publish gates remain predictable across asset classes.

results.message: UDIM texture set has missing tiles.
results.title: UDIM texture set must not have missing tiles
results.why: Missing UDIM tiles create visible render holes or fallback texture samples. Detecting incomplete tile sets before publish prevents broken assets and wasted farm renders.

results.message: Texture dependency is not using the latest detected version.
results.title: Texture version should be latest available
results.why: Keeping texture dependencies on the latest approved version helps keep publishes consistent with current lookdev handoff.

results.message: V-Ray material displacement bounds span exceeds the configured budget.
results.title: V-Ray displacement bounds span exceeds budget
results.why: Wide V-Ray displacement bounds increase silhouette change and memory cost. Enriched displacement metadata exposes bounds span for renderer-specific policy checks.

results.message: V-Ray material has force displacement enabled.
results.title: V-Ray force displacement flag requires review
results.why: Force displacement bypasses renderer heuristics and can increase tessellation cost on farm workers. These materials need TD review before publish.

results.message: V-Ray material has displacement nodes in its network.
results.title: V-Ray material uses displacement
results.why: Displacement-linked V-Ray materials should be reviewed for farm-safe scale and subdivision settings before publish.

results.message: V-Ray material uses more textures than the configured V-Ray texture budget.
results.title: V-Ray material texture count exceeds budget
results.why: High texture counts increase IO, memory pressure, and review complexity. V-Ray enrichment tracks texture count separately from generic graph lists for renderer-specific policy checks.

results.message: V-Ray material reflection trace depth exceeds the configured budget.
results.title: V-Ray material reflection trace depth exceeds budget
results.why: High reflection trace depth increases render cost and can hide unstable material settings. Enriched V-Ray metadata exposes trace depth limits for policy review.

results.message: V-Ray material has no connected texture nodes.
results.title: V-Ray material has no connected textures
results.why: Untextured V-Ray materials are valid for debug or flat-color lookdev, but production assets usually need explicit texture coverage before publish.

results.message: Scene contains V-Ray materials but no V-Ray settings/plugin node.
results.title: V-Ray plugin node missing when V-Ray materials are present
results.why: V-Ray materials require a V-Ray settings node such as VRaySettingsNode for predictable farm rendering and policy validation.
